text: 10x Chromium Single Cell 3′ Gene Expression, v3 chemistry, Illumina paired-end — a PBMC library I expect to be clean. R1 (28 bp) = 16-nt cell barcode (3M-February-2018 whitelist) + 12-nt UMI; R2 = cDNA insert; 8-nt i7 sample index. Can you QC it as a baseline?

I extracted the expected read/library structure — open **Extracted spec** in the Files panel to review it. The gist:

- **Assay:** 10x Chromium Single Cell 3′ Gene Expression (v3/v3.1), Illumina
- **Read structure:** R1 = `cell_barcode:16` + `umi:12`; R2 = `cdna_insert`; I1 = 8-nt i7 sample index
- **Whitelist:** 3M-February-2018 (16-nt gel-bead barcodes)

Review it in the viewer, then click **Confirm spec** and I'll run QC on your reads.
Looks right — confirm and run QC.
Spec confirmed ✓ — running the QC pipeline on **your uploaded reads** now. I'll stream each stage below; the ranked diagnosis and evidence chain land when it finishes.

**QC complete — overall PASS.**
This 10x Chromium 3' v3/v3.1 library looks healthy: R1 is a clean 28 bp (16 bp barcode + 12 bp UMI), 94.9% of cell barcodes are on the gel-bead whitelist, and adapter read-through and poly-G tails are all well under threshold. The only mild flag is that 3.8% of R2 reads start with the TSO — a small background of empty/short-insert products — but it stays below the 5% action limit. No intervention needed; proceed to alignment and standard cell-calling QC.

All checks passed — the reads are consistent with the expected structure.

The full report (root cause + suggested fix per issue) is open in the viewer. Any computationally-fixable issue also appears in the **Computational fixes** panel below — tick them and Apply to clean the reads and re-score.
